item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh my 343 custom theme is blue oh mY 343

+1 for theme

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

That's a perfectly reasonable headcanon! A couple people suggested that as a plausible explanation during the crit phase, actually. My intent didn't quite follow through - I meant it more as a display of Foundation negligence and covering-of-asses more than malicious experimentation, though you could argue that after a certain amount of people go Type Green, there's no practical difference. But yeah, I meant it as the Foundation covering up an SRA they left behind when they pulled out of the region (presumably after the Iranian Revolution) that then went 3005 on them. In any case, thank you for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so like… spoiler question for the author?

+ show block
– hide block

so it's pretty clear through the GOC commentary and the hints throughout the article (and obviously the closing bit) that this is the foundation covering for an SRA gone seriously, seriously messed up, and this is a massive fabricated cover story. However, it almost reads like the foundation might have honestly let this happen on… purpose? To see the effects of a failed SRA almost. Perhaps I'm reading into this whole "cover story" thing a little too much. Because the foundation is either basing their entire cover story off of making assumptions in order to fill in the blanks… or they were just observing the situation the entire time. Was this your intent? Or at the very least an acceptable headcannon in your eyes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with minmin's entire comment, including the appreciation of some of the devices used. To generalise from one criticism, I think that the implausibly poetic dialogue is just one symptom of a systemic disorder of contrivance. As another example, the needless parenthetical "illegally" and chastisement of ORIA in the main text (i.e. outside of an addendum*) felt jarringly out of place for the SCP format. Others here seem also to have noticed further instances of unsubtlety in conveying the narrative.

And this is totally aside from the writing, and I feel dumb complaining about this for two comments in a row, but the hitbox for the footnote superscripts is frustratingly small. It's not quite as bad for this as Black Highlighter–based styles, but it's still annoying.

*Thinking about it, there's something odd about the mere presence of a "conclusion" section which isn't obviously part of an addendum. I think the novelty of the CSS successfully smuggles it into the article under our noses; I'm trying to imagine it without the stylesheet, and I think it would stick out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Ex Officio Theme is available for use if you'd like to add it to your new GOC format too!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyable! Great premise and fairly well exacuted.

I personally found it a bit too obvious what was going on especially with the ending basically bashing me over the head but maybe some people didn't get it before so who am I to judge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tldr this is a good effort but some things do not sit right with me

i think the commentary part is a good idea, and i think the scenario itself is frighteningly juicy. however i cannot shake off the feeling that your voice is fighting with the format and the tone… pretty writing alone isnt what makes a piece good, it's how the affect of the language fits in the story you want to tell. the interludes in particular feel like you really wanted to write a companion piece that broke away from the cloak-and-dagger-realpolitik or goiana-lite aesthetic… that's where most of the fighting takes place, and where i feel a lot of excision is needed. or make the voice work for you, and realise this fully in another piece entirely.

(side note on the language of the interludes… i am not convinced that i am reading a translated document but i also do not know what translated Persian 'reads' like… it gives the overall effect of you trying to write the best you can from your voice, instead of properly vocalising the subaltern voices you are supposedly writing for. which is not a major issue (we are all just underage amateurs after all!) but it is something to keep in mind i think.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Epic Rounderhouse. I really like it!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Correct on all counts! And yeah, survivor autopsies is intentionally unclear phrasing - meant to imply something though I may have goofed it up.

Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
excellent. anomalous Goiânia accident. +1

spoiler
reliops

so, if I'm reading this right: it wasn't paraweaponry at all, it was the Foundation losing track of the core of an SRA, and blaming it on ORIA? and the document is partially fabricated as both a false explanation, to obscure SRA technology, and an explanation as to why they kept the GOC out (also to obscure SRA technology)?

edit: autopsies are usually performed on the dead. is the use of the term "survivor autopsies" intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing story, feels like a disaster movie like contagion where the events are slowly playing out and it’s getting worse and worse. Also always interested in anything GOC too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A very solid story. + 1

On another note: All but the 3rd footnote read like they are also GOC commentary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A marvelous story, Great GOC, Epic Rounderhouse, amazing +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very very high quality. Agreeing with every other positive comment in this thread.

My only gripe is that it could have a been a bit clearer who was speaking in some of the quotes. SCP-3352 does something very similar but is much clearer about who each quote is quoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with the above-mentioned documentary feel of the article, which I absolutely love. This mixes just the right amount of science, history, and politics, in a way that made it a thoroughly enjoyable and believable experience. This truly shows paraweaponry in a way I've never seen. The surrealist descriptions were on-point, and the slow build to that ending made it all worth it. The firsthand accounts spliced in with the narration nailed the sense of dread, and the occasional quips from the GOC laced over the document kept the stakes high. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An SCP written with commentary from the GOC overlayed to suggest inconsistencies is a brilliant idea, I was almost inclined to upvote based on that alone early on. The rest of the article certainly delivered on the concept. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: JUST LOOK AT THAT CSS THEME +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article reads like it's a documentary film, slowly revealing the horrific potential of paraweaponry. The highlighting to denote suspicion is inspired, and adds a novel twist to the article. +1. (ORIACON when?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Super cool stuff! I enjoyed the highlighting, it added a very interesting aspect of commentary and story from the GOC. Big 'ol +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
^ dumb idiot got the right ending and didn't realise it. Very good article, much enjoy.

Anyway +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation, you little piece of shit.

Definitely +1, seeing the GOC slowly dismantle the Foundation's lie was entertaining to watch and really well done. Reading back through makes the whole thing make even more sense after getting to the ending.
rounder are you a person or a goddamn typewriter you absolute genius madman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting concept! I thought that it was going to be the GOC realizing that there's a narrative element to the thing and stumbling upon pataphysics (been reading a lot of pataphysical stuff recently), but I think I like this better! Really illustrates the dynamic between the two groups.

+1 because it's fresh!

However—I feel like it was too easy to find the true explanation. This might just be a personal gripe, but I feel like this skip could be a little more complicated. Make the audience dig a little deeper for the real explanation. The angle you seem to be going for is rather heavy-handed, and while crafted beautifully, could do with just a tad more finesse.
